data.body:
=== DESIGN DOC: our-review-dimensions-v3.md ===

# rebar plan-review gate — design v3 (polyglot re-baseline + reworked anti-FP, one wave)

Supersedes v2. Folds both gap reports + both research rounds (scoping/anti-FP + prior-art diff).
Full DSO citations: dso-dimension-catalog-v2.md.

## FRAMING (corrected)
A rebar FEATURE used by a BROAD, DIVERSE CLIENT BASE — many languages, frameworks, project structures.
Judge every dimension by **cross-language generalization**, not by rebar's own codebase. (Correction: rebar is
Python with legacy bash being strangled out; the earlier "rebar is heavily bash" rationale was a conflation
with DSO and is DELETED.) Fires at open→in_progress; `claim` = fast signed-attestation check; heavy review
out-of-band; it is a REVIEW PROCESS, not a dialog — the CLIENT revises + re-runs to earn a fresh signature.
Reviews a plan an LLM/agent will EXECUTE. Acceptance criteria must be AGENT-COMPLETABLE-IN-SESSION (external
ops OK with provided auth; NO real-user/long-wait/host-live validation).

================================================================
## 0 — ARCHITECTURE: extensible dimension registry (designed to GROW)
================================================================
A dimension = a declarative **registry descriptor**, not bespoke code; the orchestrator is generic (never names
a dimension). Descriptor: `{id, name, scenario, kind(sound|judgment), exec(DET|1-TURN|2-STEP|AGENT),
routing(base|container|leaf|overlay), trigger, tools, impl(DET fn | prompt-file), checklist(binary items),
contract(verdict+findings), severity-policy, block-policy, config}`. Add/remove/tune = edit registry + drop a
prompt/check file; no orchestrator change; schema-validated for reliability. **Reuse rebar's existing
`rebar.llm` extension points** (reviewer catalog.json + contracts.py + prompt files + pluggable runner).
NOTE (prior-art): per-dimension registry + generic orchestrator is UNIQUE in the field (surveyed tools are
monolithic-holistic single prompts or hard-coded persona panels) — it's our growth substrate, and a real bet.
ONE WAVE: all dimensions ship; decomposed into appropriately-sized tasks with dependencies (no v1/deferred).
FUTURE SEAM (design-for, not built this epic): project-supplied dimensions loaded from `.rebar/` config;
project-invariant compliance (a project's own house rules) is the first such — registry shaped to accept it.

================================================================
## 1 — SHARED BASE: anti-FP contract for PLAN review (reworked; every judgment dim inherits)
================================================================
DROPPED (code/diff-review-specific, don't transfer): cite-or-omit-as-universal-gate; reachable-harm bar.
REPLACEMENTS (evidence-grounded — AbsenceBench 2506.11440, CheckEval, TICKing 2410.03608, Agent GPA 2510.08847):
- **Binary-checklist decomposition.** Each dimension = pre-registered binary yes/no checks, NOT 1-5 scalar
  scores (scalar trait scores are LESS human-aligned; binary checks raise agreement, cut variance, and make
  omission-detection tractable). E.g. "Names a failing test per AC: Y/N", "Provides a rollback step: Y/N".
- **MISSING-vs-WRONG routing** (LLMs are good at WRONG, bad at MISSING — AbsenceBench ~70% F1):
  - WRONG (asserts the plan/codebase contains something flawed) → **cite the plan text / codebase fact** (the
    surviving, narrow form of cite-or-omit) — reliable.
  - MISSING (omission) → **never ask open-ended "what's missing"**; pre-derive the EXPECTED-element set, test
    presence as binary checks, **ground the finding in the REQUIREMENT** that makes the element necessary
    ("AC block mandatory for type=task; absent"), apply the **anti-false-omission guard** (search the
    plan/codebase to confirm truly absent before flagging), heavier filtering + abstention (omissions default high-FP).
- **Blockingness-severity** (replaces reachable-harm): Critical = agent can't proceed / will build the wrong
  thing; Major = required element absent → rework; Minor = present-but-thin. Plan-risk is CAPPED (no P0 from
  not-yet-running code). Maps to rebar validate buckets critical/major/minor/warning.
- **Benign-reading filter** (single-pass adjudicator, NOT multi-round debate — debate rewards persuasion over
  truth, 2509.05396): "is there a plausible reading under which this isn't a problem? if yes → drop/downgrade."
- **Anti-fabrication instruction:** "Do NOT invent findings to look thorough; an empty category → 'nothing found'."
- **Calibrated abstention:** confidence per finding; surface only above a threshold calibrated on a small human
  gold set (N≈5-10 corrects systematic bias); abstain (don't fabricate) below. Track BOTH FPR and FNR (two-sided).
- **Single-question scoping + ONE coherence pass (HYBRID).** Per-dimension judges (low cross-talk/FP, actionable)
  PLUS one dedicated cross-dimension **coherence pass** whose only job is contradictions BETWEEN sections
  (e.g. testing strategy vs decomposition; sequencing vs declared dependencies) — single structured check, not debate.
- **Behavioral-test carve-out** (reframed rationale): structural-greps + command-output assertions are a
  LEGITIMATE CROSS-LANGUAGE test pattern (any stack's shell tests) — don't false-flag them as weak tests.
- Reference/exemplar grounding where a gold template/exemplar plan exists (reduces drift+FP).
VERDICT MODEL: per-dim PASS / AMBIGUOUS / FAIL. AMBIGUOUS = non-blocking → escalate to higher-tier adjudicator.
FAIL→block is per-dim, DATA-EARNED: DET/sound checks block immediately (near-0 FP); judgment dims start
escalate/advise, graduate to block only once measured FPR clears the bar (the calibration above).

================================================================
## 2 — DETERMINISTIC TOOLING (fail-open is an IRONCLAD invariant)
================================================================
solidlsp (Serena LSP, already in repo) + ast-grep backstop (P2 resolution, G1 callers, P5 shared-symbol);
syft + per-ecosystem registry HTTP + LLM-fallback (P3 deps); scc + lizard (P4 size/CCN); ast-grep/tree-sitter (G2 locus).
FAIL-OPEN CONTRACT (load-bearing for the diverse client base): capability map; unsupported lang / no server /
crash / timeout / no registry shim → `skipped` = PASS, NEVER a FAIL. Hard "missing" ONLY from a top-tier server
(pyright/gopls/rust-analyzer/tsserver/clangd); weak/absent server → "could-not-confirm". This is the ONLY reason
the deterministic tier generalizes across languages — the invariant must never be violated. Avoid stack-graphs
(archived), Glean (over-engineered); osv-scanner ≠ existence oracle.

================================================================
## 3 — DIMENSION REGISTRY (all ship; one wave)
================================================================
### Tier 0 — DET prefilter (fail-open)
- P1 Readiness shape (standardized `## Acceptance Criteria` all types)
- P2 File/symbol/import resolution (solidlsp+ast-grep)
- P3 Package existence (syft + registry + LLM-fallback)
- P4 Oversize signals (AC/file_impact counts, conjunction/CRUD verbs, layers/interfaces; scc/lizard; config-tunable)
- P5 Task-DAG validity + cross-task interference (shared file/symbol/DB-table/config-key/output-format)
- P6 AC/DD quality: compound-AND split · 9-word vague lexicon · spec-fidelity ≥80% word-overlap · verify-command
  robustness — **POLYGLOT FIX: the grep-anchor/shell-escape/argv-vs-help sub-checks are POSIX-shell-only →
  fail-open/advisory when the verify command isn't a POSIX shell invocation (don't FAIL a `pytest`/`go test`/Makefile
  command for lacking a grep anchor). The CONCEPT (verify-cmd asserts the right thing) is the polyglot part.**
- P7 Destructive/irreversible sniff → escalation to T4 (never pass/fail)

### Tier 1 — judgment, 1-TURN / 2-STEP (binary-checklist; inherit §1)
- F1 Criteria measurability & agent-in-session-completability (+ verify-cmd judgment remainder; + context-window-fit framing)
- F4 User/problem present (light content check)
- E1 Criteria↔description coherence + every-criterion-maps-to-a-step + **intra-plan terminology consistency** + **duplicate/near-duplicate requirement detection** (NEW, Spec Kit)
- E2 Ambiguity / executable-without-clarification (+ spike/research/TBD undercooked + **explicit decision-deferral = finding** — "choose an appropriate X" delegated to the executor, NEW anvil)
- E3 Intent fidelity — 2-STEP blind-restate → compare (child-vs-slice)
- E5 Testing-plan completeness (behavioral+contractual gaps, change-detector avoidance, failure-path + boundary coverage, RED-before-GREEN ordering)
- E6 Verification/termination completeness (per-step codebase-green/testable atomicity; RED→impl edge; + **end-state reachability**: does the union of steps actually reach the stated AC end-state? NEW Simmer — connects to collective-AC-sufficiency)
- G5 Decomposition judgment (too-big/should-have-children; vertical-slice vs horizontal-layer; context-window splittability; consumes P4)

### Tier 2 — judgment, AGENT (tool-grounded, primed by DET tools, fail-open; inherit §1)
- E4 Assumption/premise verification: DET hedge/assertion phrase-scan → FORCED codebase probe per named source (ban cached-context) + benign-reading filter
- G1+G2 Edit-set/scope accuracy: edit-target first (modify=verify locus / create=verify placement) → deterministic consumer/reference lookup (incl. rename/deprecate enumeration completeness); hallucinated+missing; high-blast≠fail
- G3 Child coverage — 4-bucket audit (fully/partial/uncovered/structural) + verbatim 3-condition standard + mandatory coverage map [container]
- G4 Child consistency — all 7 categories (implicit_shared_state/conflicting_assumptions/dependency_gap/scope_overlap/ordering/consumer_impact/residual_refs) [container]
- A1 Anti-slop / over-engineering: NIH (rebuilds existing) · config-surface-proliferation · gratuitous-abstraction (Rule-of-Three call-site count) — codebase-grounded (NOW SHIPS — core in surveyed tools)

### Tier 3 — triggered overlays (router-LLM selects RELEVANCE; overlay judges ADEQUACY)
- T1 Prior-art / novel-architecture justification (+ anti-repackaging, + Rule-of-Three) [web]
- T2 Empirical probe (red→green / spike)
- T3 Integration feasibility (+ CLI --help subcommand-inventory + assumed-vs-actual diff + auth-HTTPS precond + deprecated-API)
- T4 Compat/destructiveness as EXPLICIT JUSTIFIED CHOICE (bidirectional: unacknowledged breakage AND gratuitous compat/flags; expand-contract sequencing; rollback path)
- T5a Performance / T5b Reliability (blast-radius-only-lowers) / T5c Security (ambiguity-not-absence) / T5d Accessibility / T5e Maintainability (coupling + changeability)
- T6 UX non-happy-path
- T7 Documentation (new-needed + existing-invalidated + not-excessive/non-navigable + hot-path-instruction-bloat)
- T8 LLM/prompt antipatterns (bot-psychologist taxonomy)
- T9 Shared-state lifecycle (CREATE/UPDATE/CONSUME/RETIRE)

### Cross-cutting
- Router-LLM triage (overlay relevance; DET priors; recall-biased) · coherence pass (§1) · benign-reading adjudicator
  · verdict aggregation · attestation signing + invalidation-on-edit · per-dim FPR/FNR calibration.

================================================================
## 4 — DISTINCTIVE BETS (ahead of field; Opus to pressure-test whether worth the complexity)
================================================================
extensible registry+generic orchestrator · DET-tooling tier w/ fail-open (only grounded-in-tools critic found) ·
signed attestation + invalidation + close-gate enforcement · blind-restate E3 · router w/ DET priors ·
forced-probe E4 (most aggressive assumption-verification in the set) · G3/G4 child audits (no tool does the 7
cross-child modes) · verify-cmd-robustness P6 (must stay polyglot).

================================================================
## 5 — OUT OF SCOPE (confirmed)
================================================================
F2/F3 cross-ticket overlap/dependency (deterministic file-impact/link now; semantic backlog-scan → follow-on) ·
propose-multiple-then-select loop (authoring) · cross-service deploy topology (not in-session-verifiable) ·
DSO dogfooding/staged-rollout/7-day validation (violates agent-completable) · all-equal-5 / scalar-trait gating
(use binary checks + dimension VALUES) · structured-I/O inter-step handoff contract (authoring concern) ·
project-supplied dimensions (FUTURE seam, design-for not built).

## OPEN
- Per-dim block-policy = data-earned (DET blocks now; judgment dims escalate→block on measured FPR). Confirmed-direction.
- Coherence-pass + binary-checklist decomposition are NEW since v2 — Opus review to pressure-test.
